ops.title: Track FD design (v3, adversarially validated) — fusekit-holder on daemonkit drain: dead-owner adoption
ops.body:
# Track FD Design v3 — fusekit-holder on daemonkit drain: dead-owner adoption

**Task:** fusekit 32df2a6 (design-first — no implementation). **Supersedes:** /tmp/fd-design-v2.md (architecture VALIDATED by the v2 adversarial audit; mechanism defects fixed here). **Governing contract:** daemonkit cc-notes doc 67f6e62. **Acceptance criteria 187740e / a1758f1:** pending by design; the p6 VM + cask-upgrade run is the proof.

**What this pass is:** a targeted rework of v2's mechanism against the 10 source-provable defects the v2 audit found, plus its sub-findings. The architecture is settled and unchanged: **dead-owner adoption for every retiring lane** (the old holder drains and exits; a successor boots and adopts the dead generation via `ScanPeers`/`AdoptDead`; no live `drain.Transition` handoff), and **spec-journal-as-boot-authority** (the canonical journal is ownership-only and never roots replay). Sections v2 got right are carried verbatim or by reference; every reworked section cites re-verified source.

**Naming note (binding):** the wire field the task calls "Reason" is `RetireDeferredReason` (fusekit `mountd/protocol.go:299-300`); `RetireSkew` (`mountd/server.go:57-60`) produces the *internal* skew reason string that feeds it. The two journals are the **spec journal** (fusekit `mountd/journal.go` — mount/bridge payloads) and the **canonical journal** (daemonkit `drain.Journal` — ownership rows), throughout.

---

## 0. Verification base

v2's §0 facts all re-verified 2026-07-19 and carried. New facts this pass is built on (each re-verified against source today):

- **Strike persistence today is wholesale, not append.** Each tick's strike is recorded in the in-memory `proc.Strikes` first, then `persistStrikes(times)` atomically writes the **entire** times list (`selfretire.go:158-167,204-212`). A persist that fails leaves the in-memory strike counted; the *next successful persist writes the full list including every previously-unpersisted time*. The threshold decision (`struck`) is computed from the in-memory strikes, persisted or not, and the park lands even when the persist failed (`selfretire.go:162-176`). This is the exact semantics the audit's F5 trace demands (t1,t2 unpersisted + t3 persisted ⇒ three strikes, park, no sweep) — and it reshapes the Q1 design (§4): the retire lane needs a wholesale-write store surface, not `Gate`.
- `StrikeStore.Gate` has the 3-return signature `(allowed, until, err)`; its only in-tree destructuring caller is `SpawnGate` (`drain/run.go:95,142-155`). Neither is changed by this design (§4, §8).
- A `Transition` failure anywhere after `claimTransition` runs the deferred `abortTransition(false)` — `transitioning` cleared, **`draining` left true** (`drain/transition.go:78-85,215-219`). Only the conflicting-claim path reopens (`abortTransition(true)`, `transition.go:206-213`). An unhandled a–e failure therefore strands intake closed — the defect §2.2 fixes.
- `advanceTransition` enforces strict `step == current.Step+1` progression (`drain/journal.go:604-607`), and `decodeTransition` rejects any persisted `Step > StepSpawn` (`journal.go:561-563`). A new `StepExitArmed` step value is therefore doubly incompatible; §2.2 encodes external mode as a **record field**, not a step.
- `releaseTransition` silently succeeds when the claim is absent **or names a different generation**; it errors only on owner mismatch for a matching generation (`drain/journal.go:636-651`). `CompleteExternal` therefore needs strictly more validation (§8 D2), not "the same checks".
- `lease.Fence.Release` returns one undifferentiated `error`: it unlinks under the held lock, then closes; a close failure is returned bare (swallowing any unlink error), an unlink failure is returned wrapped (`lease/lease.go:216-231`). No sentinel or type distinguishes them — the v2 composite-fence contract that branched on that distinction was fiction. §2.3 fixes the lease contract instead.
- Mux siblings share their root's lease path and claim key: `leaseDirs(dir, muxRoot)` returns `{dir, muxRoot}` for every tenant (`mountd/server.go:971-977`), `lease.Seize` is nonblocking flock on the path (`lease/lease.go:234-269`, EWOULDBLOCK for a second fd in the same process), and `Server.claim` is a process-wide inflight map that refuses any second claim of the same dir including the holder's own (`server.go:662-674`). v2's per-key seize-all self-conflicts on the second tenant; §2.1.7 dedups by path.
- `parkPendingTeardown` transfers the fence **and** claims to a detached watcher on `ErrTeardownPending` (`server.go:1387-1425`); the watcher's resolution either releases them via `resolveGone` — which for `dropJournal=true` **drops the spec entry** (`s.deregister`, `server.go:1491-1499`) — or pins them as wedge state (`storeWedge`, `watchWedgeClear` releasing later at `server.go:1589`). Two consequences: fence ownership can leave the sweep's hands mid-drain (§2.3/§2.4 model it), and async park/wedge resolution is a spec-removal site the §3.3 inventory must cover.
- `Run`'s absent-key path terminalizes a pending row **without seizing a fence** when a complete successful `Resources.Keys` scan omits the key (`drain/run.go:350-360`), and a terminalize failure after a successful Yield releases the fence and returns an **error from Run** (`run.go:427-431`) — it is not a per-key retry. A Yield failure, by contrast, is absorbed per-key: `sweepKey` logs, calls `cfg.restore(key, fence)` itself, and returns settled=false (`run.go:433-436,441-445`). §2.3's failure policy is built on these three behaviors, not on a re-Yield that the wiring never performs.
- Spec-removal call sites, enumerated (mount entries; bridges carry no canonical keys and are out of scope): `handleUnmount` main path (`deregister`, `server.go:1246`), `handleUnmount` **rowless** path (`journalUnmount` under the seized fence, `server.go:1229-1235` — and its own comment names the post-retire state it exists for), the dead-mirror remount path (`deregister` before re-setup, `server.go:807-808`), async `resolveGone(dropJournal=true)` from park and wedge-clear watchers (`server.go:1491,1589`), the shutdown/reclaim `sweep` (`deregister`, `server.go:1712-1716`, driven by `unmountAll` at `server.go:275` and `unmountOwned`), replay-drop verdicts (`journal.go:428-548`), and terminal content-retry failure. `journal.drainClean` clears **bridges only** and keeps mount entries (`journal.go:262-268`) — it is not a mount-spec-removal site.
- **The dev lane never retires.** `appSkew` returns no-skew for a `"dev"` or empty compiled version (`selfretire.go:367-372` — `SkewCheck`'s guard), so a `proc.Spawn`'d dev-binary holder never enters the retire path at all. And the KeepAlive relauncher is **cask-owned**, installed by the cask postflight (`cmd/holder --install-launchagent`, `cmd/holder/launchagent.go:12-18`, targeting the stable bundle path `mountd.HolderApp`); `open -g -W` attaches to an already-running instance and relaunches on its real exit (CHANGELOG 0.38.3, service/keepalive.go golden). A cask-bundle holder is therefore launchd-covered **regardless of who launched it** — consumer `EnsureRunning` (`mountd/spawn.go:52-77`, `open -g` via `proc.AppLaunchNew`) included. §1 rebuilds the lane story on these two facts.
- `RemoteHost` op surface (dev-lane audit item): `Setup`/`AddMount`/`AddBridge` call `ensureRunning` (`remote.go:88,103,116`); `Teardown` short-circuits to success with **no holder contact** when the local probe reads not-mounted, and spawns otherwise (`remote.go:155-160`); `RemoveMount` and `RemoveBridge` never spawn and map `ErrHolderUnavailable` to no-op success (`remote.go:186-190,131-135`). §1's residual-window note quantifies what that means post-drain.
- `handleHealth` reports `RetireStrikes` as the in-memory `s.strikes.Times()` and `ParkedUntil` from the `retireMu` mirror, zero disk I/O (`server.go:486-505`) — the §4 mirror design reproduces this surface mechanically.

---

## 1. The architectural decision (Q2) — carried, with the lane story corrected

**Cask retirement is a dead-owner-adoption workflow, not a live handoff** — unchanged from v2 §1: the retiring holder gates, closes intake, settles, snapshots canonical into a generation, drains every mount under held fences, and exits; `service.AppKeepAlive`'s `open -g -W` LaunchAgent (untouched, byte-for-byte) relaunches the installed build; the successor adopts (socket flock → `ScanPeers`/`AdoptDead` → spec replay). No pre-exit successor, no `SpawnSuccessor`, no drain listener, no `proc.Spawn` anywhere in the retire path. fusekit never uses `StepBindListener`/`StepReleaseLock`/`StepSpawn`.

**Corrected lane story (replaces v2's reason 1, which the audit proved structurally false).** v2 claimed consumer `EnsureRunning` is "an *external* relauncher, exactly like launchd" for a dev/cask-less lane. It is not: launchd KeepAlive is unconditional and automatic, while `EnsureRunning` fires only on active consumer ops (`Setup`/`AddMount`/`AddBridge`, and `Teardown` only when the mount still reads mounted — `remote.go:88,103,116,155`); health checks and `RemoveMount`/`RemoveBridge` never spawn. Nothing bounds a lazily-relaunched lane's down-time. That claim is **retracted**, and the design no longer needs it:

- **Retirement is bound to the launchd-covered lane, by construction.** The only skew detector fusekit ships compares a *versioned* compiled build against the installed cask bundle, and its dev/empty guard means a dev build never skews (`selfretire.go:367-372`). The KeepAlive agent is installed by the cask itself and watches the stable bundle path (`cmd/holder/launchagent.go:12-18`), so every holder that *can* skew — a versioned cask-bundle instance, whether launchd- or `EnsureRunning`-launched (`open -g` either way) — has launchd as its unconditional relauncher. **The dev/`proc.Spawn` lane never retires**; this design promotes that from an accident of the version guard to a pinned invariant (P2 test: a holder with compiled version `dev`/empty never sets `Retiring` regardless of installed bundle). A versioned binary deliberately run outside the cask (the VM harness) supplies its own relauncher, as `scripts/vm/scenarios/*` already model — that is a harness contract, not a fleet configuration.
- Consequence: the audit's either/or (bound the dev-lane gap, or drop adoption for the dev lane) is dissolved at the root — there is no dev-lane retirement to bound. Adoption machinery still runs at every boot in every lane (a scan over an empty drain layout is a no-op), so a dev holder that crashes *while adopting nothing* stays trivially correct.
- **Named residual (pre-existing, unchanged by this design):** in the exit→relaunch window (seconds, launchd-bounded), a consumer `Teardown` of a drained mount short-circuits on the local not-mounted probe and reports success without removing the spec entry (`remote.go:155-160`), and `RemoveMount`/`RemoveBridge` no-op on `ErrHolderUnavailable` (`remote.go:186,131`) — the successor then replays an entry the consumer was told was removed. Today's cask retire (journal kept, exit, relaunch) has byte-identical semantics in the same window; this design neither widens the window nor the behavior. Fixing it means contacting (and possibly spawning) a holder for state-only cleanup, a consumer-visible semantics change to `Teardown`'s deliberate "retreat" short-circuit — flagged for the contract author as its own decision, out of this design's scope.

v2's reasons 2 and 3 (declining live handoff dissolves the S2/S4/S5/S9/J5-cross-process defect families; one protocol means one crash matrix) stand. The two dominant constraints carry over: (a) the frozen retire wire, every value-level delta gated behind operational goldens (§4.7); (b) fork-storm safety — zero spawns in the retire path, every spawn elsewhere gated (§5).

---

## 2. The cask retire lifecycle

### 2.1 The retire tick pipeline (order is normative)

Per tick at `now`, replacing `retirer.tick` (`selfretire.go:115-202`) step-for-step:

1. **Park check** (mirror, §4.1): parked ⇒ return. No skew check, no deferral update (`selfretire.go:117-120` preserved).
2. **Skew check** (`RetireSkew`, unchanged). Error ⇒ log-deduped return. Not skewed ⇒ clear `retiring`, reset ladder level only (mirror-only, §4.2), clear deferral, return.
3. **Deferral probe** (`leaseGateBusy`, verbatim — `selfretire.go:235-250`): busy ⇒ `retiring=false`, publish `RetireDeferredDir` + skew reason, return, no strike (F7). Note this probe also reads **watcher- and wedge-held fences** as busy (they hold the same lease files), so a post-abort holder quiesces in the deferred state — serving normally, striking nothing — until an in-flight parked teardown or wedge resolves. That is today's exact behavior and it is load-bearing for §2.4.
4. **Strike + persist** — the mirror strikes and the times persist wholesale, exactly today's block (`selfretire.go:155-185`, mechanism §4.3/§4.4): `struck` ⇒ park + `retiring=false` + STORM-BREAKER log + return, no sweep (F4). Persist failure ⇒ sweep deferred, `retiring=false`, return (F5) — an unrecorded attempt must not kill-cycle. **One strike per tick that reaches this step, exactly as today** — including ticks that resume a failed attempt (§2.2's disposition of the audit's strike-burn point).
5. **Set `retiring`** + log "draining", today's CAS + message.
6. **Transition (external mode, §2.2)**: resume the retained generation if one exists, else mint fresh (`NewGeneration` error checked; single-segment name `retire-<pid>-<unixnano>`). Steps a–e run; the claim is durably retained at `Step=StepTruncate, Mode=external`. **Any failure ⇒ `Intake.Reopen()` + `retiring=false` + serve normally; the generation and claim are retained and next tick resumes them** (§2.2). Nothing is seized yet, so nothing needs releasing.
7. **Seize-all, deduplicated, over the snapshot** (§2.1.7 below). Any failure ⇒ the §2.4 abort (the attempt is already durable).
8. **Sweep**: `drain.Run` with the cask wiring (§2.3); first hard Yield failure cancels Run's ctx and aborts (§2.4); a Run error (terminalize/canonical I/O) also routes to §2.4.
9. **Exit guard + complete**: after Run returns zero-pending, re-read canonical. **Any pending canonical row ⇒ do not exit**: `drain.CompleteExternal` + `Intake.Reopen()` + `retiring=false`; the next tick starts a fresh attempt whose snapshot includes the live rows. No pending rows ⇒ `CompleteExternal`, `retired.Store(true)`, `triggerShutdown()`. The spec journal is preserved (`retired` short-circuits nothing here — `unmountAll` runs over an already-empty registry and `drainClean` is bridge-only, `server.go:275,289-296`, `journal.go:262-268`). This guard is the successor of today's final inflight/registry check (`selfretire.go:349-356`): it closes both the audit's C1/C2 race (§2.1.7) and the resumed-attempt hole where mounts admitted **between** attempts survive as canonical-newer rows the resumed (already-snapshotted) generation would otherwise exit under.

#### 2.1.7 Seize-all: after Settle/snapshot, deduplicated by path

**Placement (fixes the audit's C1/C2).** v2 seized before the transition; a mount admitted just before `retiring` flipped could be absent from the pre-transition enumeration yet finish during Settle and land in the snapshot with no pre-held fence — the race today's final check closes (`selfretire.go:349-356`). v3 seizes **after** Transition a–e, over exactly the **snapshot keys** (the generation journal's rows — the authoritative key set, produced after Settle's real barrier). Since admission spans the whole mount op (§3.2) and the snapshot happens after Settle, every snapshotted key's mount is fully landed when seize-all runs; no snapshotted key can be mid-`Setup`. A seize failure aborts via §2.4 — heavier than v2's cheap pre-transition unwind, but rare (the step-3 probe already screened; the exposure is the probe→seize window) and correct.

**Dedup (fixes the audit's C4 self-conflict).** Seize-all computes, over the snapshot keys' spec entries, the **union** of lease paths (`leaseDirs(m.Dir, m.MuxRoot)` flattened — a shared mux root appears once) and the union of claim dirs (each tenant dir + each mux root once), then seizes each lease path once (`lease.Seize`) and takes each claim once (`s.claim`), sorted for determinism; all nonblocking, so no deadlock is possible — any refusal aborts. State held in the **fence table**: per path `{fence *lease.Fence, refs, transferred}`, per key `{paths, claims, state ∈ held|settled|transferred}`. A shared root path's fence releases only when its refcount drains — i.e. when the **last** tenant referencing it settles — so root exclusion outlives each individual tenant's settle (v2's per-key release would have dropped it mid-sweep).

**Called-out behavior delta:** claims are held for the whole sweep, so an owner unmount/reclaim of a not-yet-swept dir answers retryable `ClassBusy` for the sweep's duration rather than only during its own key's teardown window (today's sweep claims per-key transiently). The busy class and retry contract are unchanged; only the window widens, and only while an actual drain is in flight. Pinned in a P2/P5 test, not silently changed.

### 2.2 External-mode transition — the required daemonkit change, re-specified

**No `StepExitArmed`.** The audit is right twice over: `advanceTransition` enforces strict `+1` progression (`journal.go:604-607`) and `decodeTransition` rejects `Step > StepSpawn` (`journal.go:561-563`), so a new step value breaks both the progression and old readers; and it is unnecessary — in external mode, **a durable claim at `Step=StepTruncate` with `Mode=external` already means a–e are done**. The encoding:

- `transitionRecord` gains an additive `Mode` field (`"external"`; absent decodes as spawned — old records unaffected; `decodeTransition` unchanged). `claimTransition` for an existing claim additionally requires the mode to match, refusing a spawned-mode re-claim of an external claim and vice versa with `ErrDrainInProgress`.
- `TransitionConfig` gains the successor mode: **spawned** (current a..h, default) vs **external**. External validation: `BindDrainListener`/`ReleaseLock`/`SpawnSuccessor` must be nil (non-nil is a config error). External `Transition` runs a–e and returns after the `StepTruncate` advance with the claim **retained** — no steps f/g/h, no `markComplete`, no `releaseTransition`. Postcondition godoc: "the generation owns every snapshotted row; the caller drives `Run` to zero pending and then exits (an external supervisor relaunches; the successor's boot scan adopts and releases) or aborts via `CompleteExternal`". The canonical flock and socket release by process death — the point of the mode, not an omission.
- **`drain.CompleteExternal(ctx, canonical, generationName, self)`** releases the claim for the alive-owner call sites (§2.1.9, §2.4). **Strictly more validation than `releaseTransition`** (which silently succeeds on an absent claim or foreign generation, erroring only on owner mismatch — `journal.go:636-651`, fine for the spawned path's crash-retry, unsafe as an external-mode completion): CompleteExternal **errors** unless the claim is present ∧ `Generation` matches ∧ `Owner` matches ∧ `Mode==external` ∧ `Step==StepTruncate` — each violation a distinct wrapped error, because each names a different impossible state (absent: double-complete; foreign generation: two drainers; wrong mode/step: caller bug).
- **`Intake.Reopen()`** clears `draining` + `transitioning` — used by the failure path below and the §2.4 abort. (Today's only reopen is inside a failed `Transition`'s conflicting-claim branch, `transition.go:206-213`.)

**The a–e failure path (fixes the audit's S5 strand).** A `Transition` failure after the claim lands runs the deferred `abortTransition(false)`, leaving `draining=true` (`transition.go:78-85,215-219`) — under v2 the holder then bounced every new mount until the next tick, and while parked, for the whole park. v3 pins the failure contract: **on any external-mode `Transition` error, the holder immediately calls `Intake.Reopen()`, clears `retiring`, and serves normally; the generation and its durable claim are retained, and the next tick resumes them** — `claimTransition` returns the recorded phase for the matching generation+owner+mode (`journal.go:567-592`), a–c replay unconditionally (re-covering anything admitted while reopened — `transition.go:244-262`), d/e re-run or skip by phase. Rows admitted between attempts are safe in every interleaving: a pre-snapshot resume re-snapshots them; a post-snapshot resume leaves them canonical (the scoped truncate deletes only snapshot-matching rows, and their seqs sit above the preserved high-water — `journal.go:313-342`), where the §2.1.9 exit guard refuses to exit under them and the follow-up fresh attempt sweeps them.

**Strike accounting for retries — deliberate disposition of the audit's "doesn't consume another strike".** Each tick that reaches step 4 strikes, including a–e-failure resumes. This is today's pinned accounting, kept on purpose: the strike lands *before* the sweep and counts attempts, not successes — "in-process aborted sweeps count too" (`selfretire.go:155-158`), and today's persist-failure path likewise strikes in memory on every failing tick and parks by the third (`selfretire.go:177-185`, transcript #4). Three consecutive a–e failures therefore park a **serving** holder for one ladder step — the storm breaker doing precisely its job on a broken transition, with zero consumer impact (a–e touches no mounts). What the audit's finding actually broke — burning strikes while *stranded closed* — is fixed by the reopen contract above; the strike-per-tick itself is today's behavior and is pinned by transcript, not removed. (If the contract author wants strike-per-generation instead, that is a wire-visible policy change — `RetireStrikes` would grow slower — and must land as its own pinned delta; this design does not take it.)

### 2.3 The sweep: `drain.Run` cask wiring

`Generation`/`Canonical` as before; `Tick` default; `CanonicalAlive` constant `Undetermined`, `Ready` constant `true`, `Spawn` panics — all unchanged from v2 §2.3, API-blessed via D4. The `mountResources` adapter, re-specified against the real Run wiring:

- **`Keys`**: registry snapshot (`snapshotRegistry`, `server.go:1619-1628`). Enumeration failure returns the error (`run.go:380-390` honors it — never zero candidates).
- **`Seize(key)`**: returns the key's composite fence view from the §2.1.7 table. A key in state `settled` or `transferred` **errors loudly** — with abort-on-first-hard-failure no reachable path re-seizes one (superseded and absent keys never reach `sweepKey`), so a call is a wiring bug, not a state to serve. This replaces v2's "returns the already-held (possibly already-released) entry", which the audit correctly killed.
- **Composite fence (fixes the audit's C3), on a corrected lease contract.** The v2 adapter branched on a close-vs-unlink error distinction `lease.Fence.Release` cannot express (one generic error — `lease/lease.go:216-231`). Rather than infer, **the lease contract is narrowed (new fusekit change, §9 P4): `Fence.Release` returns an error only when the close — the lock release — fails; a failed unlink is best-effort GC, logged, never an error.** This is truthful to the layer's own semantics: after a successful close the lock is gone, and a stale unlocked lease file is already first-class — `Seize`'s `fdIsPath` loop retries through release-unlink races (`lease.go:234-269`) and `Probe` reads lock truth, not file existence (`lease.go:271-283`). The unlink-under-held-lock ordering is unchanged. On that contract the composite fence is honest: `Held()` = every member path's fence held; `Release()` (idempotent) decrements each path's refcount, lease-releases at zero for non-transferred paths, releases the key's claims, and **panics on any member Release error** — post-narrowing that error *means* the lock may still be held, the unreleasable state the `drain.Fence` contract (`drain/resources.go:34-42`) mandates a panic for. D8's godoc fix (a bare `*lease.Fence` does not satisfy `drain.Fence`) stands.
- **`AttestIdle(key)`**: `IdleConfirmed` iff the key's fences are held and the dir is not parked/wedged (`parkedDirs`/`wedged`). The EX seize is the busy proof (`selfretire.go:296-299` today).
- **`Yield(key)`**: graceful only — `drain(dir)`, `Host.Teardown`, registry row dropped, spec entry kept (today's `dropJournal=false`). Force sites unreachable from Yield (v2's C6 narrowing stands). Three outcomes:
  - **Clean**: registry row dropped; return nil. `sweepKey` terminalizes and releases (`run.go:424-431`).
  - **Hard refusal** (EBUSY/wedge-class error, teardown call returned): return the error. Run logs, **auto-restores the key itself** (`run.go:433-436` → `cfg.restore`), and the adapter records the hard failure and cancels Run's ctx — abort-on-first-hard-failure, reproducing today's abort-on-first-refusal instead of drain's per-key backoff (which would strand yielded keys down with no successor alive). The §2.4 abort must therefore treat this key as **already restored** — Restore is idempotent (below).
  - **Pending** (`fusekit.ErrTeardownPending`): the unmount call has NOT returned; releasing anything is the P-8 violation. Yield transfers the key's fences and claims to the park watcher exactly as today's sweep does (`parkPendingTeardown(..., dropJournal=false, ...)`, `server.go:1387-1425`), marks the key and its paths `transferred` in the fence table, and returns a distinguished error. Run's automatic `cfg.restore` for this key is a **no-op by the transferred flag** (releasing or remounting under an in-flight unmount is exactly what the flag exists to prevent); the adapter then cancels ctx and §2.4 runs with the key excluded from release and remount. The watcher owns resolution: gone ⇒ registry row dropped, spec kept, fences+claims released (`resolveGone`, dropJournal=false); still-mounted ⇒ wedge pins them (`storeWedge`/`watchWedgeClear`). Until resolution, the step-3 probe reads the watcher/wedge-held lease busy and **defers every subsequent attempt without a strike** — today's exact quiescence.
- **`Restore(key, fence)`**: idempotent, and a no-op for `transferred` keys. Otherwise: release the key's leases and claims via the table (refcounted; the remount path would self-deadlock on our own EX fence), then park-aware remount from the **spec** entry (`awaitPark` on `leaseDirs`, then `handleMount(m.mountRequest())` — today's abort body, `selfretire.go:262-288`; `probeMount` makes a still-mounted or still-attached key a zero-syscall no-op), then re-`Bump` canonical pending so the key is canonical-owned again. Remount failure leaves the key journaled and logged — today's "still journaled; the consumer or a successor heals it".
- **Terminalize-failure retry (fixes the audit's C5).** The real at-least-once sequence is: Yield clean → registry row dropped → `terminalize` fails → `sweepKey` releases the fence and **returns an error from Run** (`run.go:427-431`). v2's story — Run re-drives Yield — does not match the wiring: on the next pass the key is gone from `Keys()` (registry snapshot) and a **complete** successful scan routes it down the absent-key terminalization (`run.go:350-360`), which never calls Seize or Yield. v3 specifies that as the retry: a Run error routes to §2.4; if the §2.4 abort (or a retried attempt's Run) encounters the key again, it terminalizes via the absent path — fence-free, and safe here because intake is closed, the socket flock is held, and the registry cannot re-admit the key (§6 item 2's exclusion argument). **Yield idempotence remains a hard adapter contract** (drain requires it of any `Resources`; the `probeMount`/detached-tenant guards provide it and are tested by direct double-invocation at the adapter level: second call = zero teardown syscalls, spec entry untouched) — but the design no longer claims Run's wiring exercises it, and the §6.1 test is redrawn to the reachable sequence: yield-clean, terminalize-failure injection, Run error, retry ⇒ absent-path terminal, teardown-call counter still 1, spec entry intact.

### 2.4 Abort: transaction-like recovery, rebuilt on supersession

Trigger: seize-all failure (§2.1.7), first hard Yield failure, a pending-teardown transfer, or any Run error. Canonical re-registration supersedes generation rows (`ResolveOwner` — canonical wins on strictly newer seq, `journal.go:505-521`; the sweep terminalizes superseded rows without yielding, `run.go:324-341,417-423`; `adopt` skips them, `journal.go:230-236`). The procedure, per key class:

1. Cancel Run (done — it returned).
2. **Release**: release every held, non-`transferred` fence and claim via the table (reverse order). Transferred keys' fences and claims belong to their watcher or wedge entry — released by `resolveGone`/`watchWedgeClear` on resolution, never by the abort (releasing a fence whose unmount is in flight is the live-exclusion release the audit's refute proved; this is also the source-grounded resolution of v2's OPEN "wedged-mount abort" item, decided here rather than deferred to p6).
3. **Re-own**: `Bump(key, RowPending)` on canonical for **every** non-terminal generation key — yielded, unswept, hard-failed, and transferred alike (all their canonical rows were truncated into the generation at step e, so all need re-owning; `advanceSeq` over the preserved high-water guarantees strictly-newer seqs — `journal.go:193-228,313-342`).
4. **Remount**: for yielded keys only, `Restore`'s remount body (park-aware `handleMount` from spec; internal dispatch bypasses the intake gate as today's abort remounts do, `server.go:150-153`). Hard-failed keys were already restored by Run (their mounts never came down — the provider restored the handle, today's wedged-plain-mount behavior, `selfretire.go:255-259`); Restore's idempotence makes the distinction free. Transferred keys are **not** remounted — their unmount is in flight or wedged; post-resolution they sit down+spec-journaled+canonical-pending, healed by consumer Setup, the next attempt's absent-path terminal, or the successor's replay — today's parked-teardown outcome exactly.
5. **Vacuous completion**: run `drain.Run` once more — every generation row is superseded (step 3), so the sweep terminalizes all without touching a mount, hits zero pending, removes the generation.
6. `drain.CompleteExternal` releases the claim; `Intake.Reopen()`; `retiring.Store(false)`. The next tick starts fresh (new generation, fresh snapshot). Why complete-and-restart instead of retaining for retry: a retained generation skips re-snapshot by phase (`transition.go:263-282`), so the re-owned rows would stay canonical forever and the retry would drain vacuously and exit under live mounts (v2's argument, still correct — and now enforced independently by the §2.1.9 exit guard even if this reasoning were wrong).
7. **Abort-failure policy**: if any abort step fails durably (a re-own `Bump` or the vacuous Run's journal I/O), the holder does NOT strand: `Intake.Reopen()` + `retiring=false` + serve; the claim and generation persist and **the next tick resumes the same attempt** (§2.2 path — a–c replay, d/e by phase, seize-all re-runs, Run finds the partial re-own state: re-owned keys superseded-terminal, the rest swept or absent-terminaled). This makes the retained-generation resume path (§2.2) a first-class, exercised path — v2 claimed it was crash-only, which understated it; the mixed-abort cell gets a dedicated test (§11 assumption 2, promoted to specified behavior).

Crash mid-abort: any subset re-owned (adoption skips canonical-newer keys) + the rest generation-pending (adopted and replayed from spec) — every interleaving lands in the §2.5 matrix.

### 2.5 Crash matrix (updated)

Successor boot protocol unchanged (§3.4): socket flock → `ScanPeers` → open spec journal → replay → serve. Cells, by where the old holder died:

| # | Died at | Disk state | Successor recovery |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Before any claim | canonical pending rows; no generation | Scan: nothing. Spec replay remounts; canonical re-`Bump`ed per §3.2. |
| 2 | Mid a–e (claim at `Step<StepTruncate`, `Mode=external`) | generation empty or snapshotted; canonical intact or truncated per phase | Generation exists + owner dead ⇒ `AdoptDead` (`scan.go:185-241`); generation missing (pre-claimOwner) ⇒ abandoned-claim release (`scan.go:91-109`). Then spec replay. |
| 3 | Exit-armed or mid-sweep (claim at `StepTruncate`+external) | generation mixed pending/terminal; canonical truncated (+ late tombstones) | `AdoptDead` replays only pending rows; spec replay remounts everything — adopted-pending keys as carcasses/bare dirs, yielded keys as bare dirs. |
| 4 | After Run (generation removed), before `CompleteExternal` | claim active (external, truncate); generation missing; canonical empty of swept keys | Abandoned-claim release (`scan.go:91-109`); spec replay remounts all. |
| 5 | After `CompleteExternal` — the clean path | no claim; no generation; canonical empty of swept keys; spec journal full | Nothing to scan; spec replay remounts all. Clean exit and crash converge (J1 dissolved). |
| 6 | Mid-abort (§2.4) | claim active; generation mixed superseded/pending; canonical mixed | Cell 3 recovery; `adopt` skips superseded keys. |
| 7 | SIGKILL with a park watcher or wedge holding fences | fence fds died with the process; spec + canonical rows per the park's progress | The lease files unlock on fd death; replay's carcass discipline and the orphan reap cover a half-dead unmount (the shutdown path's own SIGKILL-residual note, `server.go:276-280`) — p6 cell. |

Boot-scan failure policy and the named fatal boundaries (`ErrSeqExhausted` per site, `ErrStaleGeneration` ⇒ crash) carry from v2 §2.5 verbatim.

---

## 3. Q3 — journal roles: spec replays, canonical owns

§3.1 (authority split), §3.2 (admission at dispatch spanning the whole op; canonical `Bump` in `setupAndRegister` with T-6 failure semantics; replay through the same path; late-unmount seq-safety), and §3.4 (boot order; J5 temporal exclusion; the `putMount` rollback race as a P0 fix) carry from v2 unchanged. §3.3 is reworked:

### 3.3 The removal ordering rule (J3) — complete inventory

**Rule: any path that permanently removes a mount's spec entry bumps its canonical row to `yielded` first** (one retry, then `recordPersistWarn` — never an op failure — leaving a canonical-pending orphan that self-heals at the next transition's absent-path terminal). The complete call-site inventory (verified §0; bridges exempt — no canonical keys):

| # | Site | Spec drop | Rule application |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Owner unmount, main path | `deregister` (`server.go:1246`) | teardown → bump yielded → deregister |
| 2 | Owner unmount, **rowless** path | `journalUnmount` under the seized fence (`server.go:1229-1235`) | **Not exempt** — v2's "no row existed" claim was wrong: rowless means no *registry* row; a canonical row is exactly what the states this path exists for carry (post-drain "row dropped, journal kept, kernel unmounted" — the path's own comment — and content-deferred replay entries, whose prior-life canonical row survives boot untouched because no `handleMount` re-bump ran). Bump yielded when a canonical row exists, then `journalUnmount`; exempt only when no canonical row exists. |
| 3 | Dead-mirror remount | `deregister` before re-setup (`server.go:807-808`) | bump yielded → deregister → `setupAndRegister` re-bumps pending (seq monotone). Crash between: spec gone + yielded tombstone — consistent, GC'd. |
| 4 | Async park/wedge resolution | `resolveGone(dropJournal=true)` → `deregister` (`server.go:1491`; wedge-clear at `1589`) | bump yielded → deregister, inside the watcher. Un-admitted, safe by seq (§3.2's late-unmount argument — the bump lands above the high-water). |
| 5 | Clean-shutdown / reclaim sweep | `sweep` → `deregister` (`server.go:1712-1716`; `unmountAll` `server.go:275`, `unmountOwned`) | bump yielded → deregister per dir. A clean shutdown thus tombstones canonical alongside emptying spec — boot finds no pending orphans. |
| 6 | Replay-drop verdicts | `replayDrop` → `journalUnmount` (`journal.go:428-548`) | bump yielded → drop, as v2. |
| 7 | Terminal content-retry failure | as v2 | bump yielded → drop. |
| — | `drainClean` | bridges only (`journal.go:262-268`) | out of scope by construction. |

Crash cells and the canonical-pending-orphan / yielded-tombstone GC story carry from v2 §3.3 unchanged (the orphan self-heals at the next transition's complete-scan absent path, `run.go:350-360`; tombstones GC at the next snapshot+truncate).

---

## 4. Q1 — the frozen retire wire

### 4.1 Field sourcing: the retireMu mirror (no disk I/O in health)

Unchanged in shape from v2 (`handleHealth` reads mirror + atomics only, `server.go:486-505`; F8 resolved by construction; legacy oddities pinned: expired-but-nonzero `ParkedUntil` until restart, unpruned times as stored). What changes is what stands behind the mirror:

**The mirror IS today's accounting objects — `proc.Strikes` + `proc.Ladder` under `retireMu` — kept as the in-process authority for the threshold, the park, and the wire, exactly as today (`selfretire.go:96-111,155-185`). The `drain.StrikeStore` (a new path beside the spec journal; ladder pinned `{10m,30m,1h}`) is the durable cross-generation persistence of the mirror's times — written wholesale, never the decision-maker.** The retire lane calls no `Gate`.

### 4.2 Reset semantics (F3) — mirror-only, no store surface needed

- **Process start**: fresh `proc.Ladder`, `parkedUntil` zero, times seeded from one `StrikeStore.Snapshot(ctx)` read (failed/corrupt ⇒ start fresh, logged — today's `loadStrikeTimes` tolerance).
- **Skew-clear**: `ladder.Reset()` only; `parkedUntil` untouched (the pinned wire oddity).
- The store's `Level`/`ParkedUntil` fields are **never written by the retire lane** (`PutTimes` mutates times only), so they are always zero here — which is not a loss but the exact translation of today's non-persistence of level and park. v2's `ResetLadder`/`ResetLevel` APIs (old D5) are therefore **withdrawn**: there is nothing durable to reset. Restart-after-trip and reskew produce byte-identical wire values to today by construction, not by a reset protocol.

### 4.3 Threshold semantics (F4) — no adapter, today's code path

`struck := mirror.Strike(now)`; `struck` ⇒ park (ladder), `retiring=false`, STORM-BREAKER log, **no sweep** — literally today's block preserved (`selfretire.go:155-176`), no daemonkit threshold semantics in the loop at all. Drain's admit-the-threshold-attempt `Gate` behavior remains what it is, governing only the **launch** store (§5), where the over-count tolerance is the point. v2's threshold-refusal adapter and its `Gate` extension are gone with the `Gate` dependency.

### 4.4 Persistence (F5) — wholesale write, healing by construction

After every strike: `StrikeStore.PutTimes(ctx, mirror.Times())` — an atomic, flocked, **wholesale** replacement of the stored times, the daemonkit-owned equivalent of today's `persistStrikes` (`selfretire.go:204-212`). Failure ⇒ sweep deferred, holder serves, today's DEFERRING log; the in-memory strike still counts and the third failing tick still parks (`selfretire.go:162-185`). **The audit's F5 trace now resolves to today's wire exactly:** t1 fails to persist, t2 fails, t3 succeeds ⇒ the t3 write carries `[t1,t2,t3]`; durable and mirror converge completely; the wire reports three strikes and the park; the sweep is refused. There is no "durable truth wins" resync discarding anything — the mirror is the truth and a successful persist copies all of it. Divergence exists only *while* the store is unwritable, in the direction of today's in-memory semantics, and heals fully at the first successful write. No wire change to declare; transcript #4 (§4.7) pins the exact audit trace.

### 4.5 Migration (F2) — unchanged from v2

`StrikeStore.ImportTimes(ctx, times)` seeds times only, only into a store with no strike state; fusekit parses `holder-retires.json` tolerantly at first boot, imports, deletes the legacy file. Level/park zero is the correct translation (§4.2).

### 4.6 Accounting provenance (F6) — unchanged from v2

Separate retire and launch stores; the retire path has no spawns, the launch path no retire logic; no population mixes.

### 4.7 Operational wire goldens (F1)

v2's transcripts 1-3 and 5-8 carry. Transcript 4 is sharpened to the audit's exact trace: **unwritable store for two ticks, then writable ⇒ third tick's park on the wire with three strikes, no sweep, and the store file containing all three times.** Added: **#9 — a–e failure retries** (transition failure ⇒ `Retiring` false between ticks, mounts land normally, strikes accrue per tick, third failure parks a serving holder); **#10 — whole-sweep claim window** (unmount of an unswept dir during an active drain answers `ClassBusy`; after abort it succeeds).

---

## 5. Launch gating and the fork-storm boundary — carried

v2 §5 carries verbatim (gate wired inside `mountd.Spawn`/`RemoteHost`; dual launch authority named and bounded per authority; launchd crash loop conceded ungated; the retire path has zero spawns, structurally), with one correction folded in: the §1 lane story replaces every "EnsureRunning is the dev lane's launchd" implication — `EnsureRunning` is an opportunistic accelerator on consumer ops, never a relauncher guarantee, and no retirement depends on it.

## 6. Contract checklist compliance (67f6e62)

1. **Idempotent Yield** — contract satisfied at the adapter (`probeMount`/detached-tenant no-op guards), tested by direct double-invocation; and the design's honest claim about Run's wiring is that a drained key's retry terminalizes via the absent path and never re-Yields (§2.3). The named test is the reachable sequence: yield-clean → terminalize-fail → Run error → retry ⇒ absent-path terminal, teardown counter 1, spec intact.
2. **Exclusive per-key fence** — carried from v2 (seize reused; exclusion set = full `leaseDirs`; successor-registration exclusion layered: socket-flock temporal exclusivity, kernel lease EX, closed intake; p6-verified), now with the §2.1.7 dedup making the mux exclusion set well-defined per *root*, refcounted across tenants.
3. **Gated spawn; "one StrikeStore" — RESOLVED, not open.** The contract's own text scopes it: `SpawnGate`'s godoc — "transition and babysit share one **launch-site** gate" (`run.go:142-147`) — and `TransitionConfig.SpawnSuccessor`'s "the same store Run's babysit spawns through". One StrikeStore *per launch site* (the socket), shared by every spawner of that socket — which this design satisfies with a single socket-derived launch store; the retire store gates no spawns and is not a launch-site store, so it does not violate the clause. v2's deferral to the contract author is withdrawn; if the author intended a literally-global store, that reading contradicts the godoc's own scoping and would merge `RetireStrikes` with launch accounting against F6 — the design records this interpretation as binding.
4. **New required wiring** — carried from v2 (Canonical required; `AdoptDead` only via scan; `NewGeneration` checked; `ErrSeqExhausted` fail-fast per named boundary; `ErrStaleGeneration` crash; the three wiring fixtures).

## 7. OpHello/OpHealth before the proto gate — carried

v2 §7 unchanged (server-side pre-gate dispatch for hello/health only; client-side cross-proto acceptance for those responses; pins; `TestWireRefusesProtoOne` stays green; no Shutdown op; separable from the core).

## 9. Phasing + Workflow Plan

Phase intermediates may be broken; only the final state must cohere. No shims.

- **P0 — baseline stewardship** (fusekit): `putMount` rollback race; stale `fileproviderd/appspawn.go` comment.
- **P1 — daemonkit** (§8 D1-D4, D7-D9), each test-pinned in daemonkit.
- **P2 — wire pins first** (fusekit, unmodified producer): §4.7 transcripts as characterization, including the sharpened #4 and the dev-never-retires pin (§1).
- **P3 — Q1 swap**: retire store (PutTimes/Snapshot/ImportTimes) behind the unchanged mirror accounting + migration; P2 transcripts stay green.
- **P4 — canonical registration + boot scan + lease contract** (Q3): `Bump` in `setupAndRegister`; the §3.3 inventory (all seven sites, rowless included); yielded-skip replay rule; boot order; **`lease.Fence.Release` narrowing (close-failure-only error; unlink = logged GC)** with its callers' tests adjusted.
- **P5 — the retire lifecycle swap** (Q2): pipeline reorder, external Transition, dedup seize-all, `mountResources` + composite fence + transfer flags, Run, abort, exit guard; bespoke `retireSweep` deleted; §2.5 matrix tests where unit-testable.
- **P6 — VM + cask-upgrade verification**: §2.5 cells 1-7, the §6.1 terminalize-retry sequence, raced-holder exclusion, park-across-upgrade, abort-restore under a real busy lease, parked-teardown transfer + abort + resolution, end-to-end cask upgrade. Proof for a1758f1; with P1-P5, for 187740e.
- **P7 — Hello/Health pre-gate** (§7), isolated.

**Workflow Plan.** The main agent tracks phases, dispatches lanes, adjudicates verdicts, and reports; all substantive execution fans out.

| Phase | Shape | Agents | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|
| P0-P1 | parallel | gpt-5.6-sol via codex-wrapper ×2 (bounded, decision-light) | sol finder/refuter diff review; daemonkit suite via scripts/test.sh |
| P2 | pipeline | opus xhigh ×1 (characterization tests against live producer) | transcripts green on unmodified fusekit |
| P3-P5 | pipeline (phase-gated) | opus xhigh ×1 per phase (decision-dense existing-code surgery) | P2 transcripts + phase crash-cell tests; sol refuter pass per phase |
| P6 | loop | opus xhigh VM driver ×1 | matrix checklist, each cell observed |
| P7 | pipeline | gpt-5.6-sol via codex-wrapper ×1 | cross-proto pins + full mountd suite |
| design gate (now) | pipeline | this doc → targeted adversarial re-audit ×1 | ACCEPT/REJECT on task 32df2a6 |

## 10. Disposition — the v2 audit's findings (this pass)

| Audit item | Disposition |
|---|---|
| C4 (seize-all self-conflict; watcher ownership) | **Fixed §2.1.7 + §2.3/§2.4**: union-dedup by lease path and claim dir, refcounted shared root fences released at last-tenant settle; `ErrTeardownPending` transfers marked in the fence table, abort never releases or remounts transferred keys, watcher/wedge owns resolution, step-3 deferral quiesces later attempts strike-free. |
| C5 (double-yield vs real wiring) | **Fixed §2.3**: the retry after terminalize-failure is Run's absent-path terminal (`run.go:350-360`) — fence-free, safe under closed intake + held flock; Seize of a settled/transferred key errors loudly; Yield idempotence kept as an adapter contract with a direct test; §6.1 redrawn to the reachable sequence. |
| S5 (a–e failure strand + strike burn) | **Fixed §2.2**: pinned failure contract — `Intake.Reopen()` + `retiring=false` + serve on any external-Transition error; generation+claim retained and resumed (mode-matched re-claim, a–c replay, d/e by phase); between-attempt admissions safe in both resume orders, backstopped by the §2.1.9 exit guard. Strike-per-tick **retained deliberately** as today's pinned breaker semantics (`selfretire.go:155-158,177-185`), with the strike-per-generation alternative named as a wire-visible policy change this design declines. |
| F5 (persistence-failure mirror divergence) | **Fixed §4.4**: the mirror is the authority and persistence is wholesale (`PutTimes`), the daemonkit translation of today's `persistStrikes` — a successful write carries previously-unpersisted times, so the audit's t1/t2/t3 trace yields three strikes + park + no sweep on the wire, byte-identical to today. No "durable truth wins" resync exists to discard anything. Transcript #4 pins the exact trace. |
| C1/C2 (seize-before-Settle race) | **Fixed §2.1.7 + §2.1.9**: seize-all moved after Transition a–e, over the snapshot keys (post-Settle, the authoritative set); the admitted-mid-flip mount is in the snapshot and gets seized. The final-check equivalent survives as the canonical-pending exit guard. |
| J3 (removal inventory; rowless exemption) | **Fixed §3.3**: seven-site inventory including dead-mirror remount, async park/wedge resolution, and the shutdown/reclaim sweep; `drainClean` shown bridge-only; the rowless-unmount exemption corrected — it bumps yielded when a canonical row exists (post-drain and content-deferred states), exempt only when none does. |
| C3 (fictional error distinction) | **Fixed §2.3 + P4**: the distinction is made real at the source — `lease.Fence.Release` narrowed to error only on close failure (unlink = logged best-effort GC, already tolerated by `fdIsPath`/`Probe` semantics); the composite fence then panics on any member Release error, which now provably means an unreleasable lock. |
| D2 (CompleteExternal validation) | **Fixed §8 D2**: strictly more validation than `releaseTransition` — presence + generation + owner + mode + step, each violation a distinct error; the lenient release stays for the spawned path only. |
| D6 (Gate signature) | **Dissolved §4/§8**: the retire lane no longer consumes `Gate`; no signature change of any kind ships. |
| Dev lane (false relauncher premise) | **Fixed §1**: premise retracted; retirement bound to the launchd-covered cask lane by the version guard (`selfretire.go:367-372`, promoted to a pinned invariant) and the cask-owned KeepAlive (`cmd/holder/launchagent.go:12-18`) that covers every cask-bundle instance regardless of launcher; the dev lane never retires, so no gap exists to bound. The post-drain false-success window (`remote.go:155,186,131`) is quantified as a pre-existing, launchd-bounded residual, unchanged by this design and flagged for its own decision. |
| D1 sub-finding (StepExitArmed vs progression) | **Resolved §2.2/§8 D1**: no new step — `Mode` field; external+`StepTruncate` is the durable exit-armed state; progression and decode guards untouched. |
| "one StrikeStore" (was OPEN) | **Resolved §6.3** from the contract's own godoc scoping: one per launch site; the retire store gates no spawns. |
| Wedged-mount abort (was OPEN) | **Resolved §2.3/§2.4** from source: transfer flags; abort never releases watcher/wedge-owned fences; deferral gates subsequent attempts until resolution — matching `parkPendingTeardown`/`resolveGone`/`storeWedge` behavior today. |

**Carried v1-refute dispositions:** all of v2 §10 stands except as amended above — F3/F4/F5/F8 now rest on §4's mirror+PutTimes mechanism (same wire outcomes, simpler machinery); C1/C2/C3/C4/C5/S5/J3 as re-fixed above; the rest (F1, F2, F6, F7, S1-S4, S6-S9, J1, J2, J4-J7, D1/D2-proto) unchanged.

## 11. Assumptions to attack + OPEN items

Assumptions (falsifiable against source or the p6 VM):

1. **Seize-all excludes mid-sweep busy** — every session lease-acquisition path goes through the lease files the union-seize pre-holds, and every op that could touch a swept dir goes through the pre-held claims. (Unchanged from v2; now over the deduped union.)
2. **The mixed-abort resume cell** — an abort that fails after re-owning a subset resumes correctly next tick (§2.4.7): re-owned keys read superseded, the rest sweep or absent-terminal. Promoted from v2's assumption to specified behavior; needs its dedicated test (P5/P6).
3. **Settle terminates** — parked work transfers after the handler returns (`done()` fires; the watcher holds claims/fences, not admission); verify against `parkPendingTeardown`'s transfer point (`server.go:1403-1432`).
4. **`Bump` on a truncated key never collides with a retried scoped truncate** — reserved-seq + high-water (`journal.go:29-34,313-342`); assumed, not re-derived.
5. **Adopted-pending replay treats still-mounted old-generation mounts as carcasses correctly** — relies on the orphan reap for a server that outlived its holder; p6 cells 3/7.
6. **Launch-store path derivation from `Socket` is stable across consumers** sharing a socket.
7. **The `Mode` field is genuinely additive** — no drain reader treats an unknown `transitionRecord` field as corruption (JSON decode ignores unknown fields; verify no strict-decode path exists).

OPEN (genuinely deferred, none load-bearing for the core protocol):

- **OPEN (p6)**: carcass/orphan timing under adoption (assumption 5's VM observation).
- **OPEN (p6)**: `retireAbortParkWait` adequacy inside Yield's pending-transfer path vs the abort's 8s.
- **OPEN (fleet)**: post-rollout deletion of the fusekit-side legacy strike-file import (§4.5) — tracked as a follow-up task.
- **Flagged decision (not this design's)**: the pre-existing post-drain false-success window in `RemoteHost.Teardown`/`RemoveMount`/`RemoveBridge` (§1 residual) — closing it changes consumer-visible "retreat" semantics.

## Citation errata (v3 re-audit, gpt-5.6-sol xhigh, 2026-07-19)

Verdict: **ACCEPT** — all ten mechanism resolutions hold against live source; zero substantive defects; the architecture (dead-owner adoption, spec-as-boot-authority) and every reworked section are sound. Every claim was re-derived at the *corrected* source locations below and still holds. These six line-citation offsets change no disposition; some inline cites above carry the pre-correction numbers:

- **C5 (run.go swap):** the two refs are backwards — `run.go:427-431` = Yield hard-fail (absorbed via `cfg.restore`, returns false/nil); `run.go:433-436` = terminalize-fail after a clean Yield (fence released, **error returned from Run**). Read §0/§2.3/§10 the other way.
- **C1/C2:** the `retireSweep` final check is `selfretire.go:353-358` (not 349-356): `late := len(s.inflight)>0 || len(s.registry)>0` — an inflight/registry check today, mapped to the new canonical-pending exit guard (a structural analogy, not a literal carry-over).
- **D1:** the strict `+1` progression check is `journal.go:608-610` (not 604-607; 604-607 is the idempotency early-return). `decodeTransition`'s `Step>StepSpawn` guard at `:561-563` is exact; Step constants Truncate=5, Spawn=8; `Mode` is genuinely additive (plain `json.Unmarshal`, no unknown-field rejection).
- **S5:** `abortTransition(reopen bool)` is `transition.go:78-85`; its only current caller (conflicting-claim branch) is `:208-215` (not 206-213); the `Reopen(true)` path `:216-220` (not 215-219). The §2.1.9 exit guard is genuinely new (Run's only completion check today is zero generation-pending at `run.go:278-283`).
- **Dev lane:** `appSkew`'s dev/empty guard is `selfretire.go:377-378` (not 367-372); the `cmd/holder/launchagent.go:12-18` cite is exact.

Two audit reinforcements: **C3** is stronger than stated — `lease.Fence.Release()` returns `error` while `drain.Fence.Release()` (`resources.go:34-42`) has *no return value*, so the error→panic adapter is a **mandatory syntactic requirement**, not stylistic; and the "unlink failure logged, never an error" narrowing needs new logging plumbing (the `lease` package has zero logging today). **Dev-lane retraction** rests on a deployment invariant, not a source-enforced one — a versioned binary run outside the cask path *could* skew without launchd coverage; v3 names this and scopes it as a harness contract, an accepted design-first residual.
